finding.contract_claim: Parameter `error` is declared in the signature of log(message, error=False) (src/app/launcher.py line 1) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: The attributed call site, log("ok") in src/app/cli.py, does not pass `error`, so the parameter appears to always take its default False. `log` is a common name with high fan-out: src/noise.py defines an unrelated log(message) that is called locally.
finding.triage_reasoning: The sweep covered only 3 files and found no external callers passing `error`. The one confirmed call site `log('ok')` in src/app/cli.py omits the parameter. However, the function body does use `error` (it's included in the return string), and `log` is a common name that could have additional callers outside the swept files. The export surface note says `error` is not exported, but that refers to the parameter symbol — the `log` function itself may well be exported and called elsewhere. With such a narrow sweep (3 files) and a common function name, the evidence is insufficient to definitively declare the parameter dead. A broader sweep of the entire codebase for calls to `log(` from `launcher` would be needed to confirm.
